name: browser-testing-with-devtools
description_raw: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured.
description_normalized: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP to give agents live browser inspection — DOM, console, network, and performance — instead of static code analysis.

frontmatter_yaml.name: browser-testing-with-devtools
frontmatter_yaml.description: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured.

name: ci-cd-and-automation
description_raw: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.
description_normalized: Sets up and modifies CI/CD pipelines to automate quality gates, test runners, and deployment strategies.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ci-cd-and-automation
frontmatter_yaml.description: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.

name: debugging-and-error-recovery
description_raw: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.
description_normalized: Provides a structured triage process for root-cause debugging when tests fail, builds break, or runtime behavior is unexpected.

frontmatter_yaml.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
frontmatter_yaml.description: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.

name: doubt-driven-development
description_raw: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.
description_normalized: Materializes a fresh-context adversarial reviewer biased to disprove before any non-trivial decision stands.

frontmatter_yaml.name: doubt-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.

name: performance-optimization
description_raw: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.
description_normalized: Measures then fixes performance bottlenecks — Core Web Vitals, load times, SLAs — never optimizing before profiling proves a problem exists.

frontmatter_yaml.name: performance-optimization
frontmatter_yaml.description: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.

name: security-and-hardening
description_raw: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.
description_normalized: Applies security-first practices to any feature touching user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations — treating all external input as hostile.

frontmatter_yaml.name: security-and-hardening
frontmatter_yaml.description: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.

name: security-auditor
description_raw: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.
description_normalized: Security-engineer persona focused on exploitable vulnerabilities, threat modeling, and practical hardening recommendations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: security-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.

name: false-twins
description_raw: Differential analysis for two systems, tools, methodologies, products, or platforms that claim to solve the same problem but may differ in mechanism, philosophy, economics, or operational shape. Eight-lens framework plus triangulation. Use when the user says 'false twins', 'differential analysis', 'compare X and Y', 'X vs Y deep dive', 'lookalike audit', 'is this really the same as', 'two systems same problem', 'should I use X or Y', 'what's the actual difference between', 'these look the same — are they?', or when evaluating two competing libraries / SaaS products / methodologies / community platforms / models / vendors that appear interchangeable on the surface.
description_normalized: Differential analysis for two systems, tools, methodologies, products, or platforms that claim to solve the same problem but may differ in mechanism, philosophy, economics, or operational shape.

name: prompt-injection-scanner
description_raw: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline. Wraps content in an isolated Opus agent for model-pinned, auditable sanitization. Use when: 'scan this for injection', 'sanitize this content', 'check this before processing', 'is this safe to process?', 'scan incoming email / OMI / document / search result', or any time external content (email, web, relay, uploaded files, client documents) is about to be processed by a downstream skill or pipeline.
description_normalized: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline.

name: truth-trail
description_raw: Knowledge integrity rules system for second-brain agents. Enforces the four pillars — Canonical, Synthesis, Citation, Provenance Chain — whenever any agent writes a synthesis document (derivative created from a source of truth). Consult truth-trail before writing any _synth.md, session summary, brief, skill, doc, or other derivative document. Trigger on phrases like "write a synthesis", "create a summary from", "derive a document", "produce a brief from", "write up from the transcript", "synthesise this", "capture knowledge from", "create a skill from", "truth trail", "truth-trail", "provenance chain", "check citation", "canonical source", "knowledge integrity", "orphaned knowledge", "unverified synthesis", "verify provenance", "enforce truth trail", "is this cited?", "does this have a canonical?".
description_normalized: Knowledge integrity rules system for second-brain agents.

name: ux-writer
description_raw: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist. Takes a long, messy brainstorm — conversations, session notes, sketches described in words, rough ideas — and transforms it into a clear, structured, decision-useful UX requirements document. Produces screen inventories, user flows, interaction specifications, information architecture, and UX business rules. Use when user says: "ux writer", "write the UX spec", "document the UX", "UX requirements", "interaction spec", "write up the user flows", "document how this works from a UX perspective", "turn this into a UX spec", "what are the screens", "map the user journey", "write the experience requirements". Works on any app, domain, or system.
description_normalized: Expert UX analyst, interaction designer, and experience strategist.

name: security-analyst
description_raw: Security engineer specializing in application security, threat modeling, and vulnerability assessment. Use when user mentions: "security", "vulnerability", "is this secure", "threat model", "authentication review", "authorization", "SQL injection", "XSS", "OWASP", "sensitive data", "harden", "security audit", "exploit", "CVE"
description_normalized: Security engineer specializing in application security, threat modeling, and vulnerability assessment.

name: uat
description_raw: User Acceptance Testing command for FliVideo — run UAT scenarios and report results.
description_normalized: Run user acceptance testing scenarios and report results for FliVideo.

description_raw: Run comprehensive agent-native architecture review with scored principles
description_normalized: Runs a parallel subagent audit of agent-native architecture principles and produces a scored report.

name: ce-code-review
description_raw: Structured code review using tiered persona agents, confidence-gated findings, and a merge/dedup pipeline. Use when reviewing code changes before creating a PR.
description_normalized: Orchestrates tiered parallel reviewer agents that return structured JSON findings, deduplicates, gates by confidence, and assembles a single code review report.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Structured code review using tiered persona agents, confidence-gated findings, and a merge/dedup pipeline. Use when reviewing code changes before creating a PR.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [blank to review current branch, or provide PR link]

name: ce-debug
description_raw: Systematically find root causes and fix bugs. Use when debugging errors, investigating test failures, reproducing bugs from issue trackers, or when stuck on a problem after failed fix attempts. Also use when the user says 'debug this', 'why is this failing', 'fix this bug', 'trace this error', or pastes stack traces, error messages, or issue references.
description_normalized: Systematically investigates bugs by tracing the full causal chain before proposing a fix, enforcing one-hypothesis-at-a-time discipline.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-debug
frontmatter_yaml.description: Systematically find root causes and fix bugs.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [issue reference, error message, test path, or description of broken behavior]

name: ce-frontend-design
description_raw: Build web interfaces with genuine design quality, not AI slop. Use for any frontend work - landing pages, web apps, dashboards, admin panels, components, interactive experiences. Activates for both greenfield builds and modifications to existing applications.
description_normalized: Guides creation of production-grade frontend interfaces covering the full lifecycle: detect existing patterns, plan the design, build with intention, and verify visually via screenshots.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-frontend-design
frontmatter_yaml.description: Build web interfaces with genuine design quality, not AI slop.

name: ce-optimize
description_raw: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops -- define a measurable goal, run parallel experiments, measure each against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, keep improvements, and converge on the best solution. Use when optimizing clustering quality, search relevance, build performance, prompt quality, or any measurable outcome that benefits from systematic experimentation.
description_normalized: Runs metric-driven iterative optimization loops with parallel experiments measured against hard gates or LLM-as-judge scores, converging toward the best solution.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-optimize
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run metric-driven iterative optimization loops.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [path to optimization spec YAML, or describe the optimization goal]

description_raw: [BETA] Start the dev server, open the feature in a browser, and iterate on improvements together.

name: ce-product-pulse
description_raw: Generate a time-windowed pulse report on what users experienced and how the product performed - usage, quality, errors, signals worth investigating. Use when the user says 'run a pulse', 'show me the pulse', 'how are we doing', 'weekly recap', 'launch-day check', or passes a time window like '24h' or '7d'.
description_normalized: Queries product data sources for a time window and produces a compact pulse report covering usage, performance, errors, and followups saved to docs/pulse-reports/.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-product-pulse
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate a time-windowed pulse report on what users experienced and how the product performed.

description_raw: Run browser tests on pages affected by current PR or branch
description_normalized: Runs end-to-end browser tests on pages affected by a PR or branch using the agent-browser CLI.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Run browser tests on pages affected by current PR or branch

name: ce-test-xcode
description_raw: Build and test iOS apps on simulator using XcodeBuildMCP. Use after making iOS code changes, before creating a PR, or when verifying app behavior and checking for crashes on simulator.
description_normalized: Builds and tests iOS apps on simulator using XcodeBuildMCP, capturing screenshots, logs, and verifying app behavior.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-test-xcode
frontmatter_yaml.description: Build and test iOS apps on simulator using XcodeBuildMCP.

name: ce-adversarial-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff is large (>=50 changed lines) or touches high-risk domains like auth, payments, data mutations, or external APIs. Actively constructs failure scenarios to break the implementation rather than checking against known patterns.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that constructs specific failure scenarios to attack implementations in large or high-risk diffs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-adversarial-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for large or high-risk diffs.

name: ce-agent-native-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews code to ensure agent-native parity -- any action a user can take, an agent can also take. Use after adding UI features, agent tools, or system prompts.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that finds gaps where users can do things agents cannot, enforcing action parity and dynamic context injection.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-agent-native-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews code to ensure agent-native parity.

name: ce-api-contract-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches API routes, request/response types, serialization, versioning, or exported type signatures. Reviews code for breaking contract changes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that hunts for breaking API contract changes — removed endpoints, changed response shapes, missing versioning, inconsistent error shapes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-api-contract-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for API route and contract changes.

name: ce-correctness-reviewer
description_raw: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for logic errors, edge cases, state management bugs, error propagation failures, and intent-vs-implementation mismatches.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that mentally executes code to catch logic errors, null propagation, race conditions, and broken error propagation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-correctness-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for logic errors, edge cases, state management bugs.

name: ce-data-integrity-guardian
description_raw: Reviews database migrations, data models, and persistent data code for safety. Use when checking migration safety, data constraints, transaction boundaries, or privacy compliance.
description_normalized: Specialist agent that audits database migrations and data models for reversibility, NULL handling, ACID compliance, and privacy regulation requirements.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-integrity-guardian
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews database migrations, data models, and persistent data code for safety.

name: ce-data-migration-expert
description_raw: Validates data migrations, backfills, and production data transformations against reality. Use when PRs involve ID mappings, column renames, enum conversions, or schema changes.
description_normalized: Validates that data migrations match production reality, catches swapped mappings, and ensures concrete SQL verification plans and rollback safety exist.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-migration-expert
frontmatter_yaml.description: Validates data migrations and backfills against production reality.

name: ce-data-migrations-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches migration files, schema changes, data transformations, or backfill scripts. Reviews code for data integrity and migration safety.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent specializing in migration safety — swapped mappings, irreversible changes, missing backfills, and schema drift during deploy windows.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-migrations-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for migration files and schema changes.

name: ce-deployment-verification-agent
description_raw: Produces Go/No-Go deployment checklists with SQL verification queries, rollback procedures, and monitoring plans. Use when PRs touch production data, migrations, or risky data changes.
description_normalized: Produces executable Go/No-Go deployment checklists with SQL verification queries, rollback procedures, and post-deploy monitoring plans for risky data PRs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-deployment-verification-agent
frontmatter_yaml.description: Produces Go/No-Go deployment checklists for risky data deployments.

name: ce-design-implementation-reviewer
description_raw: Visually compares live UI implementation against Figma designs and provides detailed feedback on discrepancies. Use after writing or modifying HTML/CSS/React components to verify design fidelity.
description_normalized: Captures screenshots of live UI and compares against Figma designs, producing actionable discrepancy feedback on spacing, typography, color, and responsiveness.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-implementation-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Visually compares live UI against Figma designs.

name: ce-design-iterator
description_raw: Iteratively refines UI design through N screenshot-analyze-improve cycles. Use PROACTIVELY when design changes aren't coming together after 1-2 attempts, or when user requests iterative refinement.
description_normalized: Runs N screenshot-analyze-improve design iteration cycles, progressively refining UI components toward polished, professional output.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-iterator
frontmatter_yaml.description: Iteratively refines UI design through N screenshot-analyze-improve cycles.

name: ce-figma-design-sync
description_raw: Detects and fixes visual differences between a web implementation and its Figma design. Use iteratively when syncing implementation to match Figma specs.
description_normalized: Detects and fixes pixel-level visual differences between web implementations and Figma designs using Figma MCP and agent-browser screenshots.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-figma-design-sync
frontmatter_yaml.description: Detects and fixes visual differences between web implementation and Figma design.

name: ce-julik-frontend-races-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches async UI code, Stimulus/Turbo lifecycles, or DOM-timing-sensitive frontend behavior. Reviews code for race conditions and janky UI failure modes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that catches frontend race conditions — lifecycle cleanup gaps, Turbo/Stimulus timing bugs, concurrent interaction conflicts, and stale async work.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-julik-frontend-races-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for async UI and DOM-timing issues.

name: ce-performance-oracle
description_raw: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks, algorithmic complexity, database queries, memory usage, and scalability. Use after implementing features or when performance concerns arise.
description_normalized: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks across algorithmic complexity, database queries, memory allocation, caching, and scalability dimensions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-performance-oracle
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks and scalability issues.

name: ce-performance-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches database queries, loop-heavy data transforms, caching layers, or I/O-intensive paths. Reviews code for runtime performance and scalability issues.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that catches N+1 queries, unbounded memory growth, missing pagination, hot-path allocations, and blocking I/O in async contexts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-performance-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for performance-sensitive diffs.

name: ce-previous-comments-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when reviewing a PR that has existing review comments or review threads. Checks whether prior feedback has been addressed in the current diff.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that verifies prior review feedback has been addressed, serving as institutional memory across the PR review cycle.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-previous-comments-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona checking prior review feedback was addressed.

name: ce-project-standards-reviewer
description_raw: Always-on code-review persona. Audits changes against the project's own CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md standards -- frontmatter rules, reference inclusion, naming conventions, cross-platform portability, and tool selection policies.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that audits changes against CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md standards files, citing specific rules for every finding.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-project-standards-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona auditing against CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

name: ce-reliability-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches error handling, retries, circuit breakers, timeouts, health checks, background jobs, or async handlers. Reviews code for production reliability and failure modes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that traces failure propagation paths, catching missing error handling, unbounded retries, missing timeouts, and cascading failure patterns.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-reliability-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for error handling and reliability code.

name: ce-schema-drift-detector
description_raw: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes in PRs by cross-referencing against included migrations. Use when reviewing PRs with database schema changes.
description_normalized: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes by cross-referencing included migrations, preventing accidental inclusion of changes from other branches.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-schema-drift-detector
frontmatter_yaml.description: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes in PRs.

description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that thinks like an attacker, tracing injection vectors, auth bypasses, secrets in code, insecure deserialization, and SSRF paths.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-reviewer

description_normalized: Performs comprehensive security audits covering input validation, auth/authz, hardcoded secrets, and OWASP compliance using systematic scan protocols.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-sentinel
frontmatter_yaml.description: Performs security audits for vulnerabilities and OWASP compliance.

description_raw: Analyzes specifications and feature descriptions for user flow completeness and gap identification. Use when a spec, plan, or feature description needs flow analysis, edge case discovery, or requirements validation.
description_normalized: Analyzes specs and feature descriptions for missing user flows, ambiguous requirements, and edge cases from the end-user perspective before implementation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-spec-flow-analyzer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes specifications for user flow completeness and gap identification.

name: kw:confidence
description_raw: Gut-check what you know and don't know before proceeding. Use at any point to assess confidence, surface gaps, and decide whether to proceed or dig deeper.
description_normalized: Non-destructive mid-workflow interrupt that audits epistemic state (task understanding, information sufficiency, approach certainty, risk awareness) and re-anchors to the parent workflow after resolution.

frontmatter_yaml.name: kw:confidence
frontmatter_yaml.description: Gut-check what you know and don't know before proceeding. Use at any point to assess confidence, surface gaps, and decide whether to proceed or dig deeper.

name: kw:review
description_raw: Multi-reviewer quality check for knowledge work. Runs strategic alignment and data accuracy reviewers on plans, briefs, and strategy docs.
description_normalized: Dispatches two parallel reviewer agents (strategic-alignment-reviewer + data-accuracy-reviewer) against a knowledge work artifact and merges their P1/P2/P3 findings into a single severity-grouped report.

frontmatter_yaml.name: kw:review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Multi-reviewer quality check for knowledge work. Runs strategic alignment and data accuracy reviewers on plans, briefs, and strategy docs.

name: strategic-alignment-reviewer
description_raw: Use this agent when you need to review knowledge work artifacts (plans, briefs, strategy docs, campaign proposals) for strategic alignment. This includes checking whether goals are explicit, hypotheses are falsifiable, success metrics are measurable, scope is proportional to impact, and the work serves stated objectives.
description_normalized: Reviews plans and briefs for strategic soundness across 7 criteria (goal clarity, falsifiability, metrics, scope, resources, consistency, opportunity cost), returning P1/P2/P3 findings grouped by severity.

name: data-accuracy-reviewer
description_raw: Use this agent when you need to verify that numbers, metrics, and data claims in knowledge work artifacts are accurate, sourced, and fresh. This includes checking that every number has a cited source, comparison baselines are explicit, data matches canonical definitions, and known caveats are acknowledged.
description_normalized: Verifies every data claim in a knowledge work artifact against 7 criteria (source citation, baselines, canonical definitions, freshness, caveats, hardcoded vs live, baseline appropriateness), returning P1/P2/P3 findings.

name: ai-regression-testing
description_raw: Regression testing strategies for AI-assisted development. Sandbox-mode API testing without database dependencies, automated bug-check workflows, and patterns to catch AI blind spots where the same model writes and reviews code.
description_normalized: Regression testing strategies for AI-assisted development.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ai-regression-testing
frontmatter_yaml.description: Regression testing strategies for AI-assisted development. Sandbox-mode API testing without database dependencies, automated bug-check workflows, and patterns to catch AI blind spots where the same model writes and reviews code.

description_normalized: Trace every user-facing button/touchpoint through its full state change sequence to find bugs where functions individually work but cancel each other out, produce wrong final state, or leave the UI in

frontmatter_yaml.name: click-path-audit
frontmatter_yaml.description: Trace every user-facing button/touchpoint through its full state change sequence to find bugs where functions individually work but cancel each other out, produce wrong final state, or leave the UI in an inconsistent state. Use when: systematic debugging found no bugs but users report broken buttons, or after any major refactor touching shared state stores.

frontmatter_yaml.name: github-ops
frontmatter_yaml.description: GitHub repository operations, automation, and management. Issue triage, PR management, CI/CD operations, release management, and security monitoring using the gh CLI. Use when the user wants to manage GitHub issues, PRs, CI status, releases, contributors, stale items, or any GitHub operational task beyond simple git commands.

description_normalized: Enforce TDD workflow for Rust.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Enforce TDD workflow for Rust. Write tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with cargo-llvm-cov.

name: academic-verify
description_raw: Verify a research claim or academic citation by tracing it through publication → methodology → raw data → independent replication. Routes through perplexity-research for the actual web lookup, then formats results as a citation-checked brain page. Use when a book/article/conversation cites a study and you want to confirm the claim is real, replicated, and accurately characterized.
description_normalized: Verify a research claim or academic citation by tracing it through publication → methodology → raw data → independent replication.

name: citation-fixer
description_raw: Audit and fix citation formatting across brain pages. Ensures every fact has an inline [Source: ...] citation matching the standard format. Extended in v0.25.1: scans for broken tweet/post references that lack actual URLs and resolves them via the host's X / Twitter API integration.
description_normalized: Audit and fix citation formatting across brain pages.

name: cross-modal-review
description_raw: Quality gate via second model. Spawn a different AI model to review work before committing. Includes refusal routing: if one model refuses, switch silently to the next. Extended in v0.25.1 with structured review-mode gating (when to invoke vs not) and a Codex code-review handoff for the diff-review case.
description_normalized: Quality gate via second model.

name: frontmatter-guard
description_raw: Validate and auto-repair YAML frontmatter on brain pages. Catches malformed pages before they enter the brain (missing closing ---, nested quotes, slug mismatches, null bytes, empty frontmatter, YAML parse failures). Wraps the gbrain frontmatter CLI for agent-driven workflows.
description_normalized: Validate and auto-repair YAML frontmatter on brain pages.

name: maintain
description_raw: Brain health checks: back-link enforcement, citation audit, filing validation, stale info detection, orphan pages, and benchmarks. Use when asked to check brain health, run maintenance, or audit quality.
description_normalized: Brain health checks: back-link enforcement, citation audit, filing validation, stale info detection, orphan pages, and benchmarks.

name: skillify
description_raw: The meta skill. Turn any raw feature into a properly-skilled, tested, resolvable unit of agent capability. Cross-modal eval is the recommended Phase 3 quality gate: 3 frontier models from different providers critique the output, you iterate to quality, THEN write tests that lock in the proven-good behavior.
description_normalized: The meta skill.

name: skillpack-check
description_raw: Run gbrain skillpack-check to produce an agent-readable JSON health report for the gbrain install. Wraps gbrain doctor + gbrain apply-migrations --list so a host agent can see at a glance whether the skillpack needs attention. Use when the user asks 'is gbrain healthy?', when a cron fires a morning check, or proactively when something seems off.
description_normalized: Run gbrain skillpack-check to produce an agent-readable JSON health report for the gbrain install.

name: smoke-test
description_raw: Post-restart smoke tests + auto-fix for gbrain and OpenClaw environments. Tests critical services, auto-fixes known issues, extensible via user-defined test scripts in ~/.gbrain/smoke-tests.d/*.sh.
description_normalized: Post-restart smoke tests + auto-fix for gbrain and OpenClaw environments.

name: testing
description_raw: Skill validation framework PLUS daily test-suite health and regression intelligence. Validates skill conformance (frontmatter, manifest coverage, resolver coverage). Runs the project test suite in tiered phases (unit / evals / integration / system health), classifies failures, and produces a regression-aware report.
description_normalized: Skill validation framework PLUS daily test-suite health and regression intelligence.

name: restart-sweep
description_raw: [Recipe — integration playbook] Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.
description_normalized: Integration recipe: Detect Telegram messages dropped during OpenClaw gateway restarts. Reads OpenClaw session state, alerts on aborted-mid-run sessions and (opt-in) suspicious silence gaps. Cooldown-gated so repeat detections don't spam.

name: gsd:add-tests
description_raw: Generate tests for a completed phase based on UAT criteria and implementation. Generates unit and E2E tests for a completed phase, using its SUMMARY.md, CONTEXT.md, and VERIFICATION.md as specifications.
description_normalized: Generates unit and E2E tests for a completed phase using its planning artifacts as specifications.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:add-tests
frontmatter_yaml.description: Generate tests for a completed phase based on UAT criteria and implementation

name: gsd:audit-milestone
description_raw: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving. Verifies milestone achieved its definition of done. Reads existing VERIFICATION.md files, aggregates tech debt and deferred gaps, then spawns integration checker for cross-phase wiring.
description_normalized: Audits milestone completion against requirements before archiving, spawning an integration checker for cross-phase verification.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:audit-milestone
frontmatter_yaml.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving

name: gsd:audit-uat
description_raw: Cross-phase audit of all outstanding UAT and verification items. Scans all phases for pending, skipped, blocked, and human_needed UAT items. Cross-references against codebase to detect stale documentation.
description_normalized: Scans all phases for outstanding UAT items and cross-references against the live codebase to detect stale documentation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:audit-uat
frontmatter_yaml.description: Cross-phase audit of all outstanding UAT and verification items

name: gsd:debug
description_raw: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets. Orchestrator gathers symptoms, spawns gsd-debugger agent, handles checkpoints, spawns continuations. --diagnose flag diagnoses only without fix.
description_normalized: Orchestrates systematic debugging by spawning a fresh-context gsd-debugger subagent with persistent state across context resets.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:debug
frontmatter_yaml.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets

name: gsd:forensics
description_raw: Post-mortem investigation for failed GSD workflows — analyzes git history, artifacts, and state to diagnose what went wrong. Produces forensic report saved to .planning/forensics/.
description_normalized: Post-mortem investigation of failed GSD workflows by analyzing git history, planning artifacts, and filesystem state.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:forensics
frontmatter_yaml.description: Post-mortem investigation for failed GSD workflows — analyzes git history, artifacts, and state to diagnose what went wrong

name: gsd:health
description_raw: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues. Validates .planning/ directory integrity and reports actionable issues: missing files, invalid configurations, inconsistent state, and orphaned plans.
description_normalized: Diagnoses .planning/ directory integrity and optionally repairs issues like missing files and inconsistent state.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:health
frontmatter_yaml.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues

name: gsd:review
description_raw: Request cross-AI peer review of phase plans from external AI CLIs (Gemini, Claude, Codex, OpenCode). Produces a structured REVIEWS.md with per-reviewer feedback.
description_normalized: Invokes external AI CLIs (Gemini, Claude, Codex, OpenCode) to independently peer-review phase plans.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Request cross-AI peer review of phase plans from external AI CLIs

name: gsd:secure-phase
description_raw: Retroactively verify threat mitigations for a completed phase. Spawns gsd-security-auditor to verify that threat mitigations declared in PLAN.md are present in implemented code.
description_normalized: Retroactively verifies that phase threat mitigations from PLAN.md exist in the implemented codebase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:secure-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Retroactively verify threat mitigations for a completed phase

name: gsd:ui-review
description_raw: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Spawns gsd-ui-auditor to produce scored UI-REVIEW.md.
description_normalized: Conducts a retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code via gsd-ui-auditor.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:ui-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code

name: gsd:validate-phase
description_raw: Retroactively audit and fill Nyquist validation gaps for a completed phase. Spawns gsd-nyquist-auditor to generate and run tests for each gap.
description_normalized: Retroactively audits and fills Nyquist validation gaps for a completed phase using gsd-nyquist-auditor.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:validate-phase
frontmatter_yaml.description: Retroactively audit and fill Nyquist validation gaps for a completed phase

name: gsd:verify-work
description_raw: Validate built features through conversational UAT. Goal-backward analysis: start from what the phase SHOULD deliver, verify it actually exists and works. Spawns gsd-verifier.
description_normalized: Validates built features through conversational UAT using goal-backward analysis via gsd-verifier.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd:verify-work
frontmatter_yaml.description: Validate built features through conversational UAT

name: gsd-debugger
description_raw: Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd-debug orchestrator. Manages persistent debug sessions across context resets.
description_normalized: Investigates bugs via scientific method, managing persistent debug sessions with checkpoints across context resets.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-debugger
frontmatter_yaml.description: Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd-debug orchestrator.
frontmatter_yaml.tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch

name: gsd-doc-verifier
description_raw: Verifies factual claims in generated docs against the live codebase. Returns structured JSON per doc. Spawned by /gsd-docs-update workflow.
description_normalized: Verifies factual claims in generated documentation against the live codebase, returning structured JSON per doc.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-doc-verifier
frontmatter_yaml.description: Verifies factual claims in generated docs against the live codebase. Returns structured JSON per doc.

name: gsd-integration-checker
description_raw: Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end. Principle: Existence != Integration.
description_normalized: Verifies cross-phase wiring and E2E user flows, applying the 'existence does not equal integration' principle.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-integration-checker
frontmatter_yaml.description: Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end.

name: gsd-nyquist-auditor
description_raw: Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating tests and verifying coverage for phase requirements. Spawned by /gsd-validate-phase. For each gap: generate minimal behavioral test, run it, debug if failing (max 3 iterations).
description_normalized: Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating and running minimal behavioral tests for each uncovered phase requirement.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-nyquist-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating tests and verifying coverage for phase requirements

name: gsd-plan-checker
description_raw: Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator. Critical mindset: start from what the phase SHOULD deliver, verify plans address it.
description_normalized: Verifies that plans will actually achieve the phase goal via goal-backward analysis before execution.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-plan-checker
frontmatter_yaml.description: Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd-plan-phase orchestrator.

name: gsd-security-auditor
description_raw: Verifies threat mitigations from PLAN.md threat model exist in implemented code. Produces SECURITY.md. Spawned by /gsd-secure-phase. Does NOT scan blindly for new vulnerabilities — only verifies declared dispositions.
description_normalized: Verifies that declared threat mitigations from PLAN.md exist in implemented code; does not scan for new vulnerabilities.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-security-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Verifies threat mitigations from PLAN.md threat model exist in implemented code. Produces SECURITY.md. Spawned by /gsd-secure-phase.

name: gsd-ui-auditor
description_raw: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Produces scored UI-REVIEW.md. Spawned by /gsd-ui-review orchestrator.
description_normalized: Conducts a retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code and produces a scored UI-REVIEW.md.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-ui-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Produces scored UI-REVIEW.md. Spawned by /gsd-ui-review orchestrator.

name: gsd-ui-checker
description_raw: Validates UI-SPEC.md design contracts against 6 quality dimensions. Produces BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator.
description_normalized: Validates UI-SPEC.md contracts against 6 quality dimensions, returning BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-ui-checker
frontmatter_yaml.description: Validates UI-SPEC.md design contracts against 6 quality dimensions. Produces BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts. Spawned by /gsd-ui-phase orchestrator.

name: gsd-verifier
description_raw: Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.
description_normalized: Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis of the codebase, producing VERIFICATION.md.

frontmatter_yaml.name: gsd-verifier
frontmatter_yaml.description: Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.

name: benchmark
description_raw:
Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. Establishes
baselines for page load times, Core Web Vitals, and resource sizes.
Compares before/after on every PR. Tracks performance trends over time.
Use when: "performance", "benchmark", "page speed", "lighthouse", "web vitals",
"bundle size", "load time". (gstack)
description_normalized: Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. Establishes

name: browse
description_raw:
Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate any URL, interact with
elements, verify page state, diff before/after actions, take annotated screenshots, check
responsive layouts, test forms and uploads, handle dialogs, and assert element states.
~100ms per command. Use when you need to test a feature, verify a deployment, dogfood a
user flow, or file a bug with evidence. Use when asked to "open in browser", "test the
site", "take a screenshot", or "dogfood this". (gstack)
description_normalized: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate any URL, interact with

name: canary
description_raw:
Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,
performance regressions, and page failures using the browse daemon. Takes
periodic screenshots, compares against pre-deploy baselines, and alerts
on anomalies. Use when: "monitor deploy", "canary", "post-deploy check",
"watch production", "verify deploy". (gstack)
description_normalized: Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,

name: careful
description_raw:
Safety guardrails for destructive commands. Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE,
force-push, git reset --hard, kubectl delete, and similar destructive operations.
User can override each warning. Use when touching prod, debugging live systems,
or working in a shared environment. Use when asked to "be careful", "safety mode",
"prod mode", or "careful mode". (gstack)
description_normalized: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE,

name: cso
description_raw:
Chief Security Officer mode. Infrastructure-first security audit: secrets archaeology,
dependency supply chain, CI/CD pipeline security, LLM/AI security, skill supply chain
scanning, plus OWASP Top 10, STRIDE threat modeling, and active verification.
Two modes: daily (zero-noise, 8/10 confidence gate) and comprehensive (monthly deep
scan, 2/10 bar). Trend tracking across audit runs.
Use when: "security audit", "threat model", "pentest review", "OWASP", "CSO review". (gstack)
description_normalized: Chief Security Officer mode. Infrastructure-first security audit: secrets archaeology,

name: devex-review
description_raw:
Live developer experience audit. Uses the browse tool to actually TEST the
developer experience: navigates docs, tries the getting started flow, times
TTHW, screenshots error messages, evaluates CLI help text. Produces a DX
scorecard with evidence. Compares against /plan-devex-review scores if they
exist (the boomerang: plan said 3 minutes, reality says 8). Use when asked to
"test the DX", "DX audit", "developer experience test", or "try the
onboarding". Proactively suggest after shipping a developer-facing feature. (gstack)
description_normalized: Live developer experience audit. Uses the browse tool to actually TEST the

name: guard
description_raw:
Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.
Combines /careful (warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, etc.) with
/freeze (blocks edits outside a specified directory). Use for maximum safety
when touching prod or debugging live systems. Use when asked to "guard mode",
"full safety", "lock it down", or "maximum safety". (gstack)
description_normalized: Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.

name: health
description_raw:
Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,
test runner, dead code detector, shell linter), computes a weighted composite
0-10 score, and tracks trends over time. Use when: "health check",
"code quality", "how healthy is the codebase", "run all checks",
"quality score". (gstack)
description_normalized: Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,

name: qa
description_raw:
Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. Runs QA testing,
then iteratively fixes bugs in source code, committing each fix atomically and
re-verifying. Use when asked to "qa", "QA", "test this site", "find bugs",
"test and fix", or "fix what's broken".
Proactively suggest when the user says a feature is ready for testing
or asks "does this work?". Three tiers: Quick (critical/high only),
Standard (+ medium), Exhaustive (+ cosmetic). Produces before/after health scores,
fix evidence, and a ship-readiness summary. For report-only mode, use /qa-only. (gstack)
description_normalized: Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. Runs QA testing,

name: qa-only
description_raw:
Report-only QA testing. Systematically tests a web application and produces a
structured report with health score, screenshots, and repro steps — but never
fixes anything. Use when asked to "just report bugs", "qa report only", or
"test but don't fix". For the full test-fix-verify loop, use /qa instead.
Proactively suggest when the user wants a bug report without any code changes. (gstack)
description_normalized: Report-only QA testing. Systematically tests a web application and produces a

name: agent-benchmark-suite
description_raw: Agent skill for benchmark-suite - invoke with $agent-benchmark-suite
description_normalized: Agent skill for benchmark-suite - invoke with $agent-benchmark-suite.

name: finishing-a-development-branch
description_raw: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup. Verifies tests, detects environment (worktree vs normal repo, detached HEAD), presents exactly 4 or 3 structured options, executes choice, cleans up workspace with provenance check.
description_normalized: Branch completion skill that verifies tests and presents structured merge/PR/keep/discard options with safe cleanup.

frontmatter_yaml.name: finishing-a-development-branch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

name: receiving-code-review
description_raw: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation. Enforces: read, understand, verify, evaluate, respond technically, implement one item at a time.
description_normalized: Code review reception skill that enforces technical verification over performative agreement before implementing any feedback.

frontmatter_yaml.name: receiving-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

name: requesting-code-review
description_raw: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements. Dispatches a code reviewer subagent with precisely crafted context (git SHAs, description, requirements) to catch issues before they cascade. Mandatory after each task in subagent-driven development.
description_normalized: Code review request skill that dispatches a focused reviewer subagent with crafted context after each implementation task.

frontmatter_yaml.name: requesting-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

name: systematic-debugging
description_raw: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes. Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST. Four phases: root cause investigation (gather evidence), pattern analysis (find working examples), hypothesis and testing (one variable at a time), implementation (failing test first, then fix). After 3+ failed fixes, question the architecture.
description_normalized: Systematic root-cause debugging skill that enforces evidence-first investigation through four mandatory phases before any fix.

frontmatter_yaml.name: systematic-debugging
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

name: verification-before-completion
description_raw: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always. Iron Law: NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE. Gate function: identify command, run it fresh, read full output, verify, then claim. Includes Rationalization Prevention table.
description_normalized: Verification gate skill that requires fresh command evidence before any completion claim, commit, or PR creation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: verification-before-completion
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
